_comment: Type B — Two Premises + Implicit Causal Link → Conclusion (Q8 / wind farms). No fallacy. Implicit no-storage assumption is the load-bearing gap.
source_text: Wind farms will not meet all our energy needs as the demand for electricity grows, because we cannot rely on a source that varies with the weather. We need power 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, which is why the construction of wind turbines, with their uneven output, is not the solution.

premises.text: Wind output varies with the weather (intermittent supply).
We need electricity 24 hours a day, 7 days a week (constant requirement).

assumptions.text: There is no practical way to store wind-generated energy to smooth out its intermittency.

conclusion.text: Wind turbines cannot reliably meet our energy needs and are not the solution.
